ddls:
CREATE TABLE "query_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each query parameter */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the query parameter (e.g., QueryParameter) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the query parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the query parameter's purpose */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is required (true/false) */,
  "schema" TEXT /* Data schema type of the parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter if not specified */,
  "example_value" TEXT /* Example value for the parameter */,
  "allowed_values" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of allowed values for the parameter */,
  "deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is deprecated (true/false) */,
  "min_length" INTEGER /* Minimum length for string parameters */,
  "max_length" INTEGER /* Maximum length for string parameters */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value for numeric parameters */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value for numeric parameters */,
  "regex_pattern" TEXT /* Regular expression pattern for validating string parameters */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to detailed documentation for the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API endpoint */,
  "endpoint_url" TEXT /* URL of the API endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method used (e.g., GET, POST) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the endpoint's functionality */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the API endpoint */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the endpoint */,
  "deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the endpoint is deprecated (true/false) */,
  "rate_limit" INTEGER /* Rate limit for the endpoint (requests per second) */,
  "authentication_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the endpoint requires authentication (true/false) */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to detailed documentation for the endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "endpoint_parameters" (
  "endpoint_param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each association between endpoint and parameter */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated API endpoint */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the query parameter being used */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order in which the parameter is used in the API request */,
  "required_in_path" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is required in the path (true/false) */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to detailed documentation for the parameter in the context of the specific endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_endpoint_parameters_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES query_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_endpoint_parameters_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES api_endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)
